population: 53 England Athletics Youth Talent Programme athletes; 29 endurance athletes; TARGET_DIRECT
intervention: Survey plus athlete and staff focus groups/interview on health and wellbeing monitoring co-creation
comparator: No automated-plan or coach-plan comparison
component_dose: Diary use; monitoring use; estimated entry time; cadence; mobile access; reminders; adaptive input; education; clarity; feedback usefulness
outcome_measure: Diary use; monitoring use; time-on-task preference; consent/start/completion attrition; clarity and feedback preferences
instrument_protocol: Cross-sectional survey and qualitative co-creation; product pilot should log item-level teach-back, time per step, abandonment, reminders and record completeness
time_points: Consent; survey start; survey completion; focus groups/interview
missingness: 90 survey records incomplete across consent, start and completion stages; stage-specific missingness retained
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 62% did not keep a training diary; 45% did not monitor health/wellbeing; half of diary users reported 0-5 minutes
uncertainty: Purposive single programme; preference estimates are not universal pilot thresholds
individual_response: Cadence and burden preferences varied; no individual prescription response assessed
limitations: Age 16-18 only; no automated 9.5-day candidate; no efficacy, safety or recruitment inference

population: 34 injured high-school athletes; only three track athletes; YOUTH_DIRECT
intervention: Consumer-grade wearable data collection with tutorials, sync checks, troubleshooting, GPS disabling and deidentified transfer
comparator: Two observed cohorts; no automated-plan or coach-plan comparison
component_dose: Daily device wear and data transfer over about two months
outcome_measure: Daily adherence; exclusions; protocol exit; missing wear; sync and support events
instrument_protocol: Prospective observational cohort; define field/day completeness mechanically and keep withdrawal, exit, technical failure and no-candidate distinct
time_points: Daily device observations; support and transfer events
missingness: Median adherence 93% and 95% in two cohorts with ranges and time variation; missing wear distinct from exclusion or withdrawal
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Median daily wearable adherence 93% and 95% in two cohorts
uncertainty: Small injured and compensated vendor-mediated sample; adherence percentages are contextual, not progression thresholds
individual_response: Time-varying adherence reported at cohort level; individual response not extracted
limitations: Only three track athletes; no plan explanation test; no physiological validity, safety or efficacy inference

population: 60 adolescents from three schools; YOUTH_DIRECT
intervention: Six-week wearable activity-tracker usability exposure plus focus groups
comparator: No athlete, coach-plan or automated-prescription comparator
component_dose: Device wear; comfort; design; sport-specific feedback; water-sport removal; sleep use
outcome_measure: Perceived ease/usefulness; comfort; removal; feedback specificity; sleep-use friction
instrument_protocol: Qualitative usability study; product transfer requires direct logging of task friction and teach-back of explanation specificity
time_points: Wear period and post-use focus groups
missingness: Device removal and use-context gaps described qualitatively; quantitative missingness not reported
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Devices generally described as easy/useful with multiple practical frictions
uncertainty: Non-athlete school sample; qualitative outcome; no objective completion or universal threshold
individual_response: Individual views varied qualitatively; no individual prescription response
limitations: No automated 9.5-day or coach comparison; ease cannot stand for completion, efficacy or safety

population: 1843 participants across 33 studies; YOUTH_DIRECT
intervention: Wearable activity-tracker interventions in children and adolescents
comparator: Heterogeneous study designs and implementation contexts
component_dose: Attitude; burden; intervention coherence; understanding; usefulness; charging; syncing; design; adherence
outcome_measure: Multidimensional acceptability and actual adherence across 33 studies
instrument_protocol: Systematic review; each feasibility dimension remains separate and cannot be reduced to exposure or stated ease
missingness: Technical, charging, syncing and device-use missingness heterogeneous; not pooled here
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Some studies reported adherence declining after novelty; no single pooled feasibility estimate extracted
uncertainty: Heterogeneous mostly non-athlete interventions and variable risk of bias
individual_response: Heterogeneous; individual participant response not extracted
limitations: No coach-plan comparison; acceptability is not evidence of effectiveness, safety or 9.5-day validity

population: 144 intervention adolescents from nine schools; YOUTH_DIRECT
intervention: Twelve-week multicomponent wearable technology intervention implementation evaluation
comparator: Cluster-RCT context; no automated plan or coach-plan comparison
component_dose: Tracker wear; content comprehension; weekly challenges; notifications; engagement
outcome_measure: Easy-to-understand content; easy tracker use; daily wear; weekly challenge completion; engagement decline
instrument_protocol: Mixed-method implementation evaluation; product should measure teach-back and actual component completion separately
missingness: Component-level dropout and wear decline reported; exact item missingness not extracted
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 83.3% found content easy to understand; 93.3% found tracker easy; 18.6% reported daily wear post-intervention; 36.1% completed weekly challenges
uncertainty: Inactive non-athlete sample; multicomponent; self-reported adherence
individual_response: Engagement varied and declined; no individual prescription response
limitations: Stated ease did not equal completion; no efficacy, safety or automated 9.5-day inference

population: 20 adult competitive middle- and long-distance runners; ATHLETE_INDIRECT
intervention: Six-week health and wellbeing monitoring prototype plus interviews
comparator: No youth, automated-plan or coach-plan comparator
component_dose: Weekly monitoring; item definitions; routine fit; visible output; data access and control
outcome_measure: Weekly completion; terminology clarity; routine fit; feedback; access/control expectations
instrument_protocol: Qualitative prototype study; transfer supports plain definitions, teach-back and visible access/control disclosures
missingness: One of 20 did not complete weekly monitoring; reasons not extracted
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 19/20 completed weekly monitoring
uncertainty: Small purposive adult sample; qualitative; broad age range
individual_response: Individual experiences varied qualitatively; no automated prescription response
limitations: Adult indirectness; no safety, efficacy or coach-comparison inference

population: 74 elite judo, swimming and volleyball athletes; ATHLETE_INDIRECT
intervention: Online sports-health surveillance system used for 15-42 weeks
comparator: Face-to-face communication remained complementary and authoritative
component_dose: Online responses; visible feedback; athlete communication expectations
outcome_measure: Response rate; user perception; expectation of feedback and face-to-face complement
instrument_protocol: Mixed-method pilot; product transfer logs response completeness and preserves human communication
time_points: Repeated online responses and end-user perceptions
missingness: Response rates 50-61%; cause-specific missingness not extracted
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Response rates 50-61%; participants regarded system as complementary to face-to-face communication
uncertainty: Adult/young-adult non-runner sample; early subjective perceptions
limitations: Comments do not establish prevention, efficacy or safety; system cannot replace coach authority

population: No athlete population; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: Methodological framework for feasibility-study design
comparator: Examples across acceptability, demand, implementation, practicality, adaptation, integration, expansion and limited efficacy
component_dose: Feasibility domains rather than a participant dose
outcome_measure: Acceptability; implementation; practicality; workflow integration; progression questions
instrument_protocol: Conceptual framework; first Formation pilot is restricted to observable feasibility and workflow processes
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Domains distinguished; no empirical effect estimate
uncertainty: No validated checklist or universal progression threshold
limitations: Conceptual and not youth or sport-specific; limited efficacy is excluded from first-pilot decision

population: Methods research; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: International Delphi definition of feasibility and pilot studies
comparator: RCT-preparation terminology and intended future process
component_dose: Pilot runs all or part of intended process on a smaller scale
outcome_measure: Whether and how the intended visible process can be done
instrument_protocol: Delphi consensus; Formation label is FEASIBILITY_ONLY and progression concerns observability and process
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Feasibility asks whether/how a future study can be done; no product effect estimate
uncertainty: Consensus oriented to RCT preparation and supplies no universal thresholds
limitations: Does not establish product identity, effectiveness, safety or correctness

population: Healthcare stakeholders; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: Multidisciplinary conceptual analysis of explainability in healthcare AI
comparator: Different users, roles and risk contexts
component_dose: Inputs; rules/reasons; missingness; uncertainty; human authority; disagreement
outcome_measure: Role-specific explanation needs and support for evaluation/disagreement resolution
instrument_protocol: Conceptual analysis; Formation pilot should test athlete and coach teach-back item by item
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Explanations may support evaluation and disagreement resolution; no quantified effect retained
uncertainty: No validated universal comprehension score
individual_response: User needs are role-dependent; individual participant response not assessed
limitations: Healthcare conceptual transfer only; exposure is not comprehension and does not prove decision benefit or safety

population: Healthcare decision-support methods; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: DECIDE-AI early-stage decision-support reporting guideline
comparator: Actual workflow, users, versions, modifications, errors and human-system interaction
component_dose: 17 AI-specific and 10 generic reporting items
outcome_measure: System/version identity; workflow roles; failures; modifications; deviations
instrument_protocol: Delphi/consensus reporting guideline; freeze generator, schema, explanation template and comparison rubric
time_points: Every candidate, no-candidate, technical failure and handoff event
missingness: Failures and deviations must be logged distinctly
adverse_events: Process adverse events and exceptions should be reported; no safety inference
effect_estimate: Reporting items only; no efficacy estimate
uncertainty: Reporting guideline rather than conduct standard
limitations: Clinical setting differs; coach remains authoritative and the guideline does not prove benefit or safety

population: 75 prospective noninterventional healthcare evaluations; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: Scoping review of silent trials generating outputs without influencing care
comparator: Heterogeneous silent/shadow evaluations; visibility to treating users varied
component_dose: Parallel output generation and prospective logging without care influence
outcome_measure: Prospective output logging; influence on care; reporting; patient/consumer engagement
instrument_protocol: Scoping review; Formation borrows parallel comparison but explicitly rejects concealment
missingness: Reporting and nomenclature were heterogeneous; screened-total wording inconsistency retained
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 75 included evaluations; human factors and patient/consumer engagement were sparse
uncertainty: English-language healthcare review; heterogeneous reporting
limitations: Not youth sport; conventional silent label may imply concealment; no effectiveness or safety inference

population: Children and adolescents in health-related research; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: CIOMS Guideline 17 on research involving children and adolescents
comparator: Jurisdiction-specific ethics and legal requirements
component_dose: Age/maturity-tailored information; ongoing assent; required permission; refusal and withdrawal; possible re-consent at majority
outcome_measure: Affirmative assent; parental/legal permission where required; refusal; continued participation; withdrawal
instrument_protocol: Official ethics guidance; Formation pilot needs prior local determination, ongoing assent and penalty-free withdrawal
time_points: Initial information; assent; continued participation; withdrawal; majority where applicable
adverse_events: Research adverse-event process must be locally approved; guidance does not establish product safety
effect_estimate: Ethical requirements; no intervention effect estimate
uncertainty: Global guidance; local law controls exact implementation
individual_response: Understanding and willingness may change over time
limitations: Not sport-specific; grants no recruitment authority and no product efficacy or safety claim

population: Children in research; METHOD_ONLY
intervention: UK Health Research Authority guidance on research involving children
comparator: Applicable local law and ethics review
component_dose: Appropriate language/presentation; understanding; assent; refusal; withdrawal; privacy
outcome_measure: Maximized understanding; respected refusal and withdrawal
instrument_protocol: Official UK guidance; product pilot must test comprehension rather than infer it from age
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Guidance only; no empirical effect estimate
uncertainty: UK-specific and not universal legal approval
limitations: No sport or 9.5-day validation; applicable South Korean/local review remains required

population: Children and adolescent athletes
intervention: AAP clinical report on overuse, overtraining and burnout
comparator: Clinician evaluation, counselling and individual circumstances
component_dose: Repetitive stress and insufficient recovery; pain/function trajectory; sleep, fatigue and mood concerns
outcome_measure: Current pain; functional limitation; persistent sleep/fatigue/mood concern; suspected overuse
instrument_protocol: Clinical report: explicit concern is a structured HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED input; product does not classify pain, diagnose overtraining, prescribe rest or clear participation
adverse_events: Bone, muscle and tendon overuse injury discussed; no product AE rate
effect_estimate: Repetitive stress without sufficient recovery can produce overuse injury; pain may progress in timing and limitation
uncertainty: Guidance is not a validated prediction model or exclusive standard of care
limitations: Not runner-specific; no automated symptom interpretation, safety probability, readiness or 9.5-day validation

population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
intervention: Female Athlete Triad state-of-science consensus with new adolescent model
comparator: Clinical measurement and interpretation by qualified human
component_dose: Energy deficiency; reproductive function; bone health; bone stress injury; optional explicit health concern
outcome_measure: Adolescent model components and uncertainty about complete bone-health recovery
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: sex-specific evidence is retained but only explicit privacy-reviewed concern routes to human; no inference from sex, weight, pace or note text
adverse_events: Bone stress injury incorporated; no consumer adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Model links energy deficiency, reproductive function and bone health; effect estimate not extracted
uncertainty: Complete bone-health recovery uncertain; clinical measurement required
limitations: Female-specific construct; no sex-based score, restriction, diagnosis, readiness or product safety inference

population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
intervention: Female Athlete Triad clinical consensus guideline
comparator: Multidisciplinary clinical screening, diagnosis, treatment and return-to-play
component_dose: Clinical measures and categories; no universal energy-availability threshold
outcome_measure: Explicit health concern or unresolved required field; multidisciplinary management
instrument_protocol: Guideline boundary: concern, prefer-not-answer or incompatible required record is fail-closed for candidate and routes to qualified human; no threshold, point system, menstrual/body-mass rule or return-to-play automation
missingness: Unresolved required response remains unresolved; no adverse inference from silence
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Clinical functions assigned to humans; no product effect estimate
uncertainty: No single universal energy-availability threshold; consumer validation absent
limitations: Female-specific and medical-data dependent; no diagnosis, readiness, safety or automated clearance

population: Adolescent and young adult male athletes; especially endurance and weight-class sports
intervention: Male Athlete Triad clinical consensus guideline
comparator: Multidisciplinary medical team assessment
component_dose: Energy deficiency; reproductive-axis suppression; bone health; explicit relevant concern
outcome_measure: Male-specific evidence and multidisciplinary screening/diagnosis/return-to-play boundary
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: same non-diagnostic structured concern and qualified-human route for every athlete; male sex never clears or protects
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Clinical association and management consensus; no product effect estimate
uncertainty: Smaller male evidence base; abstract only; consensus extrapolation
limitations: No youth-running software validation, diagnosis, risk score, readiness or automated return-to-play

population: Healthy children and adolescents
intervention: AASM pediatric sleep-duration consensus
comparator: Modified RAND consensus and human evaluation for persistent concern
component_dose: Regular sleep duration recommendations by age band
outcome_measure: Recommended regular sleep duration; persistent sleep concern; single-night limitation
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: regular or persistent concern may route to guardian/coach/health professional; one night's hours cannot medically block or clear a session and no readiness score is generated
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 9-12 hours for ages 6-12 and 8-10 hours for ages 13-18
uncertainty: General-health recommendations are not athlete injury, recovery or clearance thresholds
individual_response: Individual sleep needs not converted to product score
limitations: Not athlete-specific; no single-night injury prediction, diagnosis, recovery, readiness or 9.5-day safety inference

population: Athletes with acute respiratory infection; youth directness low
intervention: IOC clinical consensus on acute respiratory infection
comparator: Sport and exercise medicine clinician assessment
component_dose: Explicit current illness concern; fever/chills; chest pain; breathing difficulty; fainting; severe/unusual fatigue
outcome_measure: Infection severity, athlete health risk, activity risk and symptom monitoring as clinician functions
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: any explicit illness concern or unresolved required item suppresses candidate and routes to qualified care; product does not diagnose infection, grade severity or run a return-to-sport algorithm
adverse_events: Acute respiratory illness is source domain; no product AE rate
effect_estimate: Consensus recommendations; no effect estimate extracted
uncertainty: Youth runner directness low; accessible sections/abstract rather than open full text
limitations: Respiratory focus; no consumer symptom-checker validation, readiness, medical safety or clearance inference

population: Adolescent athletes ages 12-18; under-12 outside scope
intervention: Multidisciplinary team-physician consensus for adolescent athletes
comparator: Knowledgeable health-care assessment without discrimination
component_dose: Growth/development; medical and musculoskeletal issues; psychological factors; overtraining; return to sport
outcome_measure: Verified age/development context and explicit health concern; outside-scope or unknown context
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: missing, incompatible or out-of-scope age/development context suppresses candidate for human review; product does not estimate maturity or substitute for team physician
missingness: Unknown/out-of-scope context retained as transfer limitation
adverse_events: Injury and illness domains covered; no event rate extracted
effect_estimate: Consensus guidance; no quantified effect estimate
uncertainty: Abstract only; consensus not prediction model; under-12 excluded
limitations: No youth-runner-specific rule, diagnosis, readiness, safety probability or automated return-to-sport clearance

population: Athletes across ages and levels; children and youth explicitly considered
intervention: IOC scoping review and Delphi consensus on interpersonal violence and safeguarding
comparator: Named trained safeguarding lead and jurisdiction-approved response process
component_dose: Athlete-centred whole-system trauma- and violence-informed safeguards; visible support route
outcome_measure: Accessible concern-response mechanisms; 43 consensus statements; participant-controlled support request
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: explicit concern or REQUEST_HUMAN_SUPPORT suppresses candidate and routes content outside planner to trained safeguarding human; no narrative classification, credibility scoring or coach disclosure by default
time_points: Concern report; acknowledgement; human handoff; resolution state
missingness: Narrative detail is not required and remains outside generator; route/status may be logged only after approval
adverse_events: Interpersonal violence and safeguarding events are source domains; no product event rate
effect_estimate: 43 statements reached consensus; several did not
uncertainty: Complex subjective domain; local legal and reporting duties vary
individual_response: Individual disclosure and route remain participant-centred
limitations: No software diagnosis or abuse classification; exact local procedure, confidentiality and emergency copy require human approval

population: Children participating in sport from grassroots through elite
intervention: International organizational safeguards for children in sport
comparator: Named safeguarding owner with tested local concern-response procedure
component_dose: Eight safeguards: policy; response procedures; advice/support; risk management; behaviour; recruitment/training; partnerships; monitoring
outcome_measure: Existence and operation of a named human route before concern collection
instrument_protocol: Organizational boundary: concern collection remains fail-closed until a real safeguarding owner and tested procedure exist; software is never the safeguarding officer
missingness: Jurisdiction-specific narrative and reporting records are outside planner
adverse_events: Safeguarding process exceptions should be logged; no empirical AE rate
effect_estimate: Eight organizational safeguards; no outcome effect estimate
uncertainty: Organizational standard rather than empirical outcome study; jurisdiction-specific implementation
limitations: No diagnosis, credibility score, readiness, safety or automated case resolution; local law controls

population: Elite youth athletes across pathways; mixed-sport transfer to runners
intervention: Scoping review of associations between growth, maturation and injury
comparator: Heterogeneous cohort methods and qualified human assessment
component_dose: Growth rate; maturity status/timing; injury incidence and burden; growth-spurt context
outcome_measure: Injury incidence/burden by maturity and growth measures across 30 eligible studies
instrument_protocol: Review boundary: participant/guardian may report growth-change concern for HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; product does not estimate peak-height velocity, skeletal age, maturity or injury probability
adverse_events: Injury incidence and burden extracted narratively; no product AE rate
effect_estimate: Incidence/burden generally increased with maturity status; growth-related injury peaked around growth spurt; faster growth associated in some studies
uncertainty: Maturity timing unclear/inconsistent; heterogeneous definitions; female and sport diversity underrepresented
individual_response: Group associations varied; no individual prediction validated
limitations: Mixed-sport and elite-pathway directness; no causal threshold, diagnosis, readiness or clearance rule

population: Male elite adolescent athletics academy athletes; 117 athlete-seasons
intervention: Prospective cohort relating skeletal maturation and growth rates to injury
comparator: Within-cohort differences in growth rate and skeletal maturity
component_dose: Stature growth rate; leg-length growth rate; skeletal maturity; bone and growth-plate injury
outcome_measure: Bone-injury and growth-plate-injury incidence-rate ratios per growth measure
instrument_protocol: Study measurements require expert methods; product accepts only explicit rapid-growth or growth-related-pain concern and routes to human, never recreates maturity measures
adverse_events: Bone and growth-plate injuries prospectively observed
effect_estimate: Per 1 SD higher stature growth rate: bone-injury IRR 1.5 and growth-plate-injury IRR 2.1; leg-length growth also associated
uncertainty: 95% CI 1.1-1.9 and 1.5-3.1 respectively; multiple measures and injury types
individual_response: Individual response not established from cohort associations
limitations: Small male elite single-academy observational study; numeric IRRs cannot become individual risk score, diagnosis or clearance

population: 748 competitive high-school runners at baseline; follow-up data for 428 girls and 273 boys
intervention: Prospective cohort of sex-specific stress-fracture associations
comparator: Girls and boys analyzed separately; prior factors and follow-up injury outcomes
component_dose: Prior fracture; reported BMI; menarche timing; gymnastics/dance history; prospective stress fracture
outcome_measure: Prospective stress-fracture occurrence and sex-specific multivariable associations
instrument_protocol: Cohort boundary: only explicit prior clinician-diagnosed fracture/BSI or current concern routes to qualified human; no BMI, sex, menarche or sport-history score
time_points: Baseline factors and prospective seasonal follow-up
missingness: Follow-up available for 428 of 442 girls and 273 of 306 boys; self-reported baseline factors
adverse_events: 23 girls and 11 boys sustained prospective stress fracture
effect_estimate: 5.4% of followed girls and 4.0% of followed boys; prior fracture most robust predictor
uncertainty: Low event counts; multivariable uncertainty and exact estimates beyond summary not extracted
individual_response: Sex-specific associations differed; individual prediction not validated
limitations: Older cohort; self-report and low events; no causal threshold, diagnosis, risk probability, readiness or restriction from sex/BMI/menarche

population: High-school and collegiate cross-country runners
intervention: Systematic review of prospective running-related injury risk factors
comparator: Prospective cohorts with heterogeneous definitions
component_dose: Sex; prior injury; RED-S-related constructs; training; sleep; specialization
outcome_measure: Strength and certainty of associations with running-related injury and BSI
instrument_protocol: Review boundary: history or explicit concern may route to human but cannot diagnose recurrence; training/sleep evidence cannot drive automated readiness or risk prediction
adverse_events: Running-related injury and BSI outcomes; no pooled product AE rate extracted
effect_estimate: Sex and prior running injury were strongest predictors; moderate certainty for greater RED-S-related risk and injury, especially BSI
uncertainty: Training, sleep and specialization evidence low certainty; mixed high-school/collegiate samples
limitations: Heterogeneous definitions and transition-age population; no automated risk score, diagnosis, readiness, safety or clearance

population: Adolescent athletes
intervention: Narrative review of adolescent bone stress injury risk, diagnosis and management
comparator: Multidisciplinary clinical management and heterogeneous source populations
component_dose: Load; energy balance; bone health; biomechanics; psychological context; suspected localized bone pain; prior BSI
outcome_measure: Incidence and recurrence ranges across cited adolescent populations
instrument_protocol: Review boundary: suspected/localized bone pain, prior BSI or clinician restriction suppresses candidate and routes to qualified clinician; product does not infer BSI from load or provide management
adverse_events: Bone stress injuries across cited populations
effect_estimate: Reported incidence ranges 3.9%-19% and recurrence up to 21%
uncertainty: Narrative synthesis; minimal pediatric empirical evidence; ranges not individual probabilities
limitations: Not runner-only; no diagnosis, treatment, risk score, readiness or safety inference from training data

population: Children and adolescents in repetitive youth sports
intervention: Systematic review of primary periphyseal stress injury
comparator: Case reports/series and sparse prospective evidence
component_dose: Pain; localized tendon-attachment or bone-region symptoms; limp; functional limitation; imaging findings
outcome_measure: Growth disturbance; surgery; delayed presentation; symptomatic and asymptomatic findings
instrument_protocol: Review boundary: explicit pain, limp, limitation or uncertainty is HUMAN_REVIEW_REQUIRED; product does not classify injury, infer imaging need or diagnose apophysitis/tendon injury
adverse_events: Among 448 reported patients, 57 growth disturbances and 28 surgeries; harmful delayed presentations reported
effect_estimate: 101 of 128 reports were case reports/series; 12.7% growth disturbance and 6.2% surgery among reported patients
uncertainty: Selection/publication bias; some imaging changes asymptomatic
limitations: Mixed sports; no symptom-screen validation, causal threshold, diagnosis, treatment, readiness or clearance

population: Athletes age 19 or younger; seven studies
intervention: Systematic review and meta-analysis of sleep and sports injury
comparator: Chronic poor sleep exposure versus other sleep; acute studies considered separately
component_dose: Chronic sleep exposure; acute single-night evidence; sports injury
outcome_measure: Pooled injury association for chronic poor sleep and conflicting acute evidence
instrument_protocol: Review boundary: regular/persistent sleep concern may route to human; one poor night cannot become injury prediction, medical stop, recovery or readiness state
time_points: Study-specific; chronic and acute exposures separated
missingness: Heterogeneous self-report exposure and injury definitions
adverse_events: Sports injuries in seven included studies
effect_estimate: Random-effects OR 1.58 for chronic poor sleep and injury
uncertainty: 95% CI 1.05-2.37; two acute-sleep studies conflicted; Level IV synthesis
limitations: Only seven observational studies; no causal or individual threshold, diagnosis, readiness, safety or session-clearance inference

population: Growing children and adolescents with apophyseal or osteochondral pain
intervention: Peer-reviewed clinical review of common pain causes in growing bones
comparator: Clinical history, examination and sometimes imaging
component_dose: Explicit pain; localized tenderness; limp or altered movement; activity limitation; persistent symptoms
outcome_measure: Symptoms and clinical evaluation boundary for hip, knee and ankle growth-related sites
instrument_protocol: Clinical-review boundary: any explicit pain or limitation suppresses candidate and routes to qualified human; product asks only structured concern and never labels apophysitis, tendon injury or osteochondrosis
adverse_events: Potential serious pathology discussed; no event rate extracted
effect_estimate: Running sports can affect growth-related sites; diagnosis depends on clinical assessment
uncertainty: Much guidance expert opinion/consensus in absence of trials
limitations: Not a primary cohort; no diagnosis-specific management, treatment, risk probability, readiness or clearance inference

population: Conceptual discussion focused on female athletes
intervention: Editorial critique of the original 2014 RED-S construct
comparator: Female Athlete Triad interpretation and later evolving models
component_dose: Conceptual scope, evidence sufficiency and terminology
outcome_measure: Claim overreach and disagreement between RED-S and Triad frameworks
instrument_protocol: Editorial boundary: explicit concern suppresses candidate and routes to qualified human; evolving contested terminology prohibits automated model allegiance, label or score
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Original 2014 RED-S construct argued to overreach available evidence
uncertainty: Polemical editorial predating 2023 consensus; conceptual disagreement rather than comparative validation
limitations: Female-focused and authorship overlap; no diagnosis, score, readiness, safety or automated return-to-play inference

population: Youth athletes across participation and performance levels
intervention: IOC consensus on healthy youth athletic development
comparator: Child-centred human development and safeguarding processes
component_dose: Healthy, inclusive, sustainable and enjoyable participation; individual development; safeguarding
outcome_measure: Protection and participation benefit; individual pathways; non-participation and uncertainty
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: structured uncertainty or non-participation is respected and can suppress candidate; route to participant/guardian or named safeguarding human as appropriate
adverse_events: Injury, illness and safeguarding are broad domains; no event rate extracted
effect_estimate: Broad principles, no quantified effect estimate
uncertainty: No runner-specific dose or referral thresholds
limitations: Broad consensus; no blanket exclusion by age, sex, body size or performance and no medical clearance inference

population: Children and adolescent runners; highest target directness in packet
intervention: International consensus on minimizing injury and illness in youth runners
comparator: Individual clinical judgement and qualified human assessment
component_dose: Training, nutrition, health and participation considerations; no validated age-distance dose
outcome_measure: Evidence basis for age-distance recommendations; individualized judgement; pain, illness, nutrition and participation concerns
instrument_protocol: Consensus-informed boundary: explicit concern suppresses candidate and routes to appropriate human; age alone never clears; no diagnosis, risk score or readiness state
adverse_events: Injury and illness are source domains; no product adverse-event rate extracted
effect_estimate: Suitable running-distance recommendations by age described as opinion-based; evidence base incomplete
uncertainty: Limited mostly adolescent evidence; no validated age-distance or clearance rule
individual_response: Individual decisions required; no algorithmic individual response validated
limitations: Consensus and abstract/accessed sections only; no 9.5-day safety validation or automated medical inference

population: 113 competitive adolescent distance runners from England Athletics clubs
intervention: Retrospective online questionnaire of running-related injury and training practices
comparator: Sex and age-group descriptive comparisons; no intervention
component_dose: One questionnaire completed April-December 2018 about previous 12 months
outcome_measure: RRI incidence proportion and rate; body area; overuse mechanism; training frequency volume intensity; specialization
instrument_protocol: Cross-sectional self-report questionnaire with incidence proportion and participation-hour rate calculations
missingness: Target sample was 151 and 113 participated; item-level missingness not retained
adverse_events: Running-related injuries were the observed outcome
effect_estimate: All-RRI incidence proportion 68% and rate 6.3 per 1000 participation hours; knee foot or toes and lower leg common; 58% reported high specialization
uncertainty: 95% CI 60%-77% for incidence proportion and 5.3-7.4 per 1000 hours for incidence rate
individual_response: Training frequency increased with chronological age; individual trajectories not measured
limitations: Retrospective cross-sectional self-report; no maturity measurement; descriptive association only; cannot produce safe load injury prevention causal threshold readiness or safety inference

population: Female and male athletes exposed to low energy availability; adolescent context retained
intervention: Correction-aware 2023 IOC RED-S clinical consensus
comparator: Trained clinician and multidisciplinary team assessment
component_dose: Fueling/eating/menstrual/hormonal/recurrent bone-stress or prior clinician concern; CAT2 remains clinician-facing
outcome_measure: Explicit participant-selected concern or clinician restriction; assessment and sport-participation framework
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: explicit concern or unresolved required response suppresses candidate and routes to qualified clinical team; no CAT2 computation, RED-S label, body-size inference or return-to-play automation
adverse_events: Health consequences discussed; no product AE rate
effect_estimate: RED-S is a clinical syndrome with health and performance consequences; no universal diagnostic threshold
uncertainty: Heterogeneous evolving evidence; published correction applied
individual_response: Clinical assessment is individualized and multidisciplinary
limitations: Not youth-runner-specific; no consumer classifier, diagnosis, safety, readiness or 9.5-day prescription claim

population: Elite youth athletes approaching international competition
intervention: IOC consensus on healthy, safe and sustainable elite youth competition
comparator: Individual pathway plus coach for schedule facts and qualified clinician for health concerns
component_dose: Competition context; injury, illness, harassment and abuse protection
outcome_measure: Exact recent competition fact; incompatible schedule record; participant health concern
instrument_protocol: Consensus boundary: competition timing is factual context only; incompatible facts route to coach and explicit health concern to clinician; no elapsed-time recovery or post-competition clearance
time_points: Competition event and subsequent human review as needed
missingness: Incompatible or missing competition facts remain unresolved rather than imputed
adverse_events: Injury, illness, harassment and abuse are protection domains; no rate extracted
effect_estimate: Adolescence described as non-linear and asynchronous; no quantified Formation result
uncertainty: Elite/Olympic expert synthesis; no Formation-specific test
limitations: Indirect to ordinary youth runners; recent competition cannot become readiness, recovery, safety or next-MAIN clearance

population: Systematic-review reporting; no athlete population
intervention: PRISMA 2020 transparent review reporting framework
comparator: Incomplete search screening and exclusion reporting
component_dose: Reporting checklist and flow structure
outcome_measure: Search sources dates screening exclusions flow and amendments
instrument_protocol: Official PRISMA 2020 reporting guidance
missingness: Missing databases and inaccessible full texts must be disclosed
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Supports auditable search screening and exclusion reporting only; no sports-science effect estimate
uncertainty: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Reporting guidance does not appraise evidence establish pilot progression validate 9.5 days or support efficacy safety or prescription authority
